title: Chrysothamnus tortifolius
taxonomy: Chrysothamnus tortifolius is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Chrysothamnus, which comprises several species of shrubs native to North America. The classification of Chrysothamnus tortifolius also places it within the subfamily Asteroideae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Further, it is sometimes referred to as a synonym of Ericameria crassifolia, another species within the same family. The species is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.

intro: Chrysothamnus tortifolius, commonly known as rabbitbrush, is a shrub native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, thus providing health benefits for the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Chrysothamnus tortifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with rabbitbrush include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues.
